scope: Cross-repo audit of remaining stdlib/portable_fs migration sites — baseline for staged rollout. Builds on omega_hexa_1_portability (R1.2 stdlib helper APPLIED) and the first migrated consumer (anima_audio.hexa, anima 6e4d6fef).
approach: Read-only grep+sample audit across /Users/ghost/core. Verify the ~864 / ~743 estimates from omega_hexa_patches_applied.json against ground truth, separating active code from .claude/worktrees/ + .shared-backup-* duplicates. No code edits. Stop at JSON write.

verified_site_counts.delta_explanation: Raw counts overshoot the patches_applied estimate because they include duplicates under .claude/worktrees/agent-* and .shared-backup-20260421-022340/. Active counts (excluding both) are dramatically smaller.
verified_site_counts.compression_ratio_vs_estimate: 133 active / 1607 estimate = 8.3% — the headline 1607 was inflated ~12x by worktree+backup duplicates

distribution_by_filetype.verdict: .sh files are <1% of sites — overwhelmingly a hexa-internal migration, not a shell-shim concern

date_plus_s_N.context: fires every hexa invocation — hot path

mechanical_patterns.exec_call_pct: 104/133 = 78% — the dominant pattern is exec("...stat -f %z PATH...").trim()
self_aware_examples: stat -c %s fallback hand-rolled in airgenome scripts
mechanical_patterns.graceful_fallback_pattern: date +%s%N 2>/dev/null || echo 0 — works on Mac (returns 0) but loses all timing; partial mitigation only
mechanical_patterns.heredoc_verdict: Almost no heredoc-into-bash-into-python pattern. The high-risk class predicted in the audit prompt is essentially absent — only 2 sites use python3 -c fallback for now_ns inside .sh.

site_classes_summary.shell_script_inline: 5 of 133 (4%) — needs a bash file_size shim or `stat -c %s 2>/dev/null || stat -f %z` rewrite
site_classes_summary.stdlib_self_referential: 4 of 133 (3%) — portable_fs.hexa itself uses raw stat/date by design (cannot self-import)
site_classes_summary.performance_anchor_in_hot_path: 13 of 133 (10%) — nexus/cli/run.hexa + hexa-lang/gate/pre_tool_guard.hexa + similar — every-invocation cost matters; the file_size() wc-c overhead (~50% slower than stat) is acceptable but now_ns() exec overhead doubles per-call cost
site_classes_summary.already_dual_platform: 3 of 133 (2%) — leave as-is or migrate for uniformity

performance_envelope.wc_c_us_per_call: ~75us (POSIX, reads file via stdin pipe — still O(1) on most filesystems but fork+pipe overhead)
performance_envelope.verdict: The dominant cost is exec() itself, not the underlying stat-vs-wc choice. R1.3 (interpreter native builtin) would 40x speed up; R1.2 (current stdlib) is correct for portability but does not move the perf envelope.

now_ns_precision_floor.consequence: Timing benches that rely on intra-second resolution silently degrade to 0 deltas on Mac post-migration. Affects nexus/cli/run.hexa hold-time bench and pre_tool_guard.hexa rate limiter.

physics_or_limits.migration_safety_floor: All 4 helpers are pure additive — no breaking signature changes. Worktree/backup duplicates (94 files under .claude/worktrees, plus 5 under .shared-backup) MUST be excluded from sed scope or downstream merges will reintroduce regressions.

friction_observed.f1_estimate_inflation: patches_applied.json '864 + 743' headline counts include worktree+backup duplicates; honest active scope is 12x smaller. Future audits should grep with `-l | grep -v worktree | grep -v backup` baked in.
friction_observed.f2_self_hosted_helper: portable_fs.hexa itself contains 6 raw stat/date calls (file_size uses wc-c so escapes; now_ns + file_mtime + file_exists use stat/date directly). Cannot self-import — these 6 sites are PERMANENT exceptions, not migration targets.
friction_observed.f3_now_ns_precision_loss_silent: Mac fallback returns sec*1e9 with no warning; consumers doing (t1-t0) get 0 for sub-second intervals. No telemetry hook to detect 'all-zeros bench' state.
friction_observed.f4_partial_self_aware_sites: 3 sites already do BSD-then-GNU manual fallback. Migrating them to file_size() removes their hand-rolled robustness in favor of stdlib uniformity — net positive but worth noting in commit message.
friction_observed.f5_shell_script_orphans: 5 .sh sites cannot import portable_fs.hexa. Need a parallel bash function library (e.g., scripts/lib/portable_fs.sh exposing `file_size()` + `now_ns()`) for full coverage.
friction_observed.f6_no_regression_harness: No test currently exercises file_size on Linux containers vs Mac host with diff comparison. ω-audio empirical (anima_audio.hexa) was the first incidental detector. A 5-fixture cross-platform smoke would catch all 4 helper regressions in <30s.

omega_hexa_cont_1_hexa_stdlib_migration_.intent: Migrate the 104 in-hexa exec("stat/date...") sites to file_size/now_ns calls, phased: hot first (run.hexa, pre_tool_guard.hexa), then warm (rotate_*, gc.hexa), then cold (airgenome archive).
omega_hexa_cont_1_hexa_stdlib_migration_.predicted_diff_loc: ~150 lines net negative (each site collapses from 1 long exec line to 1 short call)
omega_hexa_cont_1_hexa_stdlib_migration_.risk: low (additive helper already in place, mechanical sed-able)

omega_hexa_cont_2_bash_shim_equivalent.intent: Author scripts/lib/portable_fs.sh exposing file_size/now_ns as bash functions for the 5 .sh sites that cannot import hexa stdlib.
omega_hexa_cont_2_bash_shim_equivalent.risk: low — bash function library is well-trodden ground

omega_hexa_cont_3_heredoc_python_migrati.intent: Originally hypothesized as a high-risk class. Empirical scope is 2 sites total. DEFER or fold into ω-cont-2.
omega_hexa_cont_3_heredoc_python_migrati.verdict: Audit prompt overweighted this class — scrap as a standalone axis.

omega_hexa_cont_4_now_ns_precision_telem.intent: Add a 'precision_floor_detected' counter to portable_fs that pings when fallback path is taken, to surface silent Mac degradation.
omega_hexa_cont_4_now_ns_precision_telem.scope: 5 LOC in portable_fs.hexa + 1 reader in pre_tool_guard.hexa

omega_hexa_cont_5_file_mtime_file_exists.intent: These 2 helpers are exposed but have ZERO consumers yet. Sweep for `stat -f %m`, `[ -f X ]`, `test -f`, and `os.path.exists`-equivalents to find natural adopters.

omega_hexa_cont_6_cross_platform_regress.intent: Author a 5-fixture smoke (small file, large file, missing file, current ns, mtime) executed once on Mac host + once in Linux container; assert non-divergent results within tolerance. Gate future portable_fs changes on green smoke.
omega_hexa_cont_6_cross_platform_regress.risk: medium — requires container exec path that ω-hexa-3/4 audits flagged as flaky

omega_hexa_cont_7_rollout_sequencing.intent: Order migrations by hottest-first (per-invocation impact) rather than per-repo. Sequence: hexa-lang/gate/pre_tool_guard (hot) -> nexus/cli/run.hexa (hot) -> anima/tool/anima_cli/* (session-warm) -> nexus/scripts/rotate_* (periodic) -> airgenome/archive/* (cold). Each phase ships an independent commit with rollback diff.
omega_hexa_cont_7_rollout_sequencing.risk: low — each phase is independently revertable

next_cycles: omega-hexa-cont-7 (rollout sequencing — author the phased commit plan)
omega-hexa-cont-6 (cross-platform regression harness — must precede or parallel cont-1 to catch regressions)
omega-hexa-cont-2 (bash shim — small but completes coverage)
omega-hexa-cont-4 (now_ns precision telemetry — surfaces silent Mac degradation)
